question_text:
Burst Balloons

You are given `n` balloons, indexed from `0` to `n - 1`. Each balloon is painted with a number on it represented by an array `nums`. You are asked to burst all the balloons. If you burst the `i-th` balloon, you will get `nums[i - 1] * nums[i] * nums[i + 1]` coins. After the burst, the `i - 1` and `i + 1` then becomes adjacent. Find the maximum coins you can collect by bursting the balloons wisely.
input_format: Space-separated values as per problem arguments.
output_format: A single line representing the result.
explanation: We use entry-based dynamic programming. `dp[left][right]` represents the maximum coins we can get by bursting all balloons between indices `left` and `right`. We assume the last balloon to burst in this range is at index `i`. Time complexity is O(N^3).
